blocks.text: When I had just started exploring the world of cinema, “Grave of the Fireflies” gave me a unique emotional experience. It helped me realize that peace and war should be examined from different points of view. It is necessary to understand the war not only through the eyes of the winners, but also through the eyes of the losers.

Isao Takahata wasn’t as famous as Hayao Miyazaki was, but I truly believe that he was the only man who could challenge the Master as a peer, rival and friend. Isao Takahata died on the 5th of April, 2018 at the age of 82, leaving us with a priceless legacy of his films and influences.
With a light heart, the whole team of Deus ETH is here to tell him: “Arigatou gozaimasu”.
